element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn 8-directional movement and gun/grenade dual-weapon system against basic infantry
Introduces vehicle mechanics with fuel management; tanks are bullet-immune but vulnerable to explosions
Enables flight over water obstacles; dual weapon system (spread gun and cannon)
Computer-initiated pressure mechanic; red targeting spot appears below player if moving too slowly upscreen
Collectible enhancements positioned strategically along progression path

typical_tension_triggers: Tracking fire mechanic (call for fire) when player lingers
Tank fuel depletion forcing player to eject
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating pressure through cumulative enemy waves and tracking fire; player agency maintained through vehicle commandeering and dual-weapon rotation system

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to 8-directional movement with rotary joystick mechanics; basic enemy infantry encounters establish foundational combat rhythm
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tank and helicopter vehicle mechanics layered in; tracking fire pressure increases to enforce forward momentum; water obstacles introduce vertical-axis variation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Cumulative enemy density and call-for-fire frequency escalates toward final objective of reaching village of Ikari to rescue Colonel Cook
notable_spikes: Introduction of tracking fire mechanic (forces pacing discipline)
Final stage approach with concentrated enemy forces

notable_level_patterns.description: Player proceeds upscreen from bottom toward village objective while lateral enemy soldier formations attack; tanks and helicopters provide temporary relief windows
notable_level_patterns.example_level: General stage structure across NES campaign

notable_level_patterns.description: Tank and helicopter placement creates pacing breaks; fuel/ammunition scarcity forces exit timing decisions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late stages where vehicle access is critical

notable_level_patterns.description: Call-for-fire mechanic intensifies throughout campaign; red targeting spot below player enforces upward movement discipline
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Becomes increasingly prevalent in late game

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Ikari Warriors NES page)
wikipedia (Ikari Warriors article - Gameplay and Development sections)
